description: Migration creates `inbound_webhooks` with tenant in composite PK
Migration creates `inbound_webhook_deliveries` with tenant in composite PK
Slug uniqueness enforced per-tenant, not globally
`lookupAlgaEntityByExternalId` resolves via `tenant_external_entity_mappings` and respects `integration_type=webhookSlug` scoping
`writeEntityMapping` inserts a row with correct unique constraint behavior; duplicate write is idempotent
Reserved integration_type collision: user webhook slug `ninjaone` rejected (or auto-namespaced) so it cannot pollute bundled integration mappings
Migration is idempotent (re-running is safe)
`inbound_webhook` permission seeded for admin role
Non-admin role does NOT receive inbound_webhook permissions by default
Zod validation rejects upsert with bogus auth_type
Zod validation rejects HMAC config without signature header name
Zod validation rejects direct_action handler without action name
Zod validation rejects workflow handler without workflow_id
Zod validation rejects URL-unsafe slug characters
`listInboundWebhooks` returns only current tenant's webhooks
`upsertInboundWebhook` rejects duplicate slug in same tenant
`upsertInboundWebhook` allows same slug across different tenants
`upsertInboundWebhook` writes secret to vault, not DB column
`getInboundWebhook` does NOT return secret in response
`rotateInboundWebhookSecret` invalidates old secret immediately
`deleteInboundWebhook` cascades to deliveries OR retains deliveries (decide and test)
Server actions reject when user lacks `inbound_webhook` permission
Server actions require tenant context (withAuth)
POST to `/api/inbound/<tenant>/<slug>` with valid HMAC returns 200 + delivery_id
POST with invalid HMAC returns 401, no delivery row beyond rejected-auth log
POST with mismatched signature header name returns 401
POST with valid Bearer token succeeds
POST with bad Bearer token returns 401
POST from IP in allowlist succeeds
POST from IP outside allowlist returns 401
POST with valid path-token succeeds
POST with invalid path-token returns 401
Unknown tenant slug returns 401 (identical to bad auth — no enumeration)
Unknown webhook slug under valid tenant returns 401 (identical body)
Disabled (is_active=false) webhook returns 401
HMAC comparison is timing-safe (no early-return on byte mismatch)
Bearer comparison is timing-safe
Headers `Cookie` and `Authorization` are stripped before persistence
Header-source idempotency key extracted from configured header
JSONata-source idempotency key extracted from configured path
Duplicate idempotency key within window returns 200 no-op
Same idempotency key after window expires triggers fresh dispatch
Missing idempotency key (when source is configured) — define behavior and test (accept vs reject)
Different webhooks with same idempotency key dedup independently
Delivery row persisted with auth_status=verified before dispatch
Auth-rejected requests log to separate row with limited detail (no body)
Dispatch failure leaves delivery row with dispatch_status=failed and error message
Rate limit: requests above per-webhook threshold get 429
Rate limit isolates per webhook (one webhook hitting limit doesn't affect another)
Rate limit isolates per tenant
Sample payload capture stores first verified body within capture window
Capture window expires after 5 min; later requests don't overwrite
`clearSamplePayload` removes the saved sample
Action registry: `registerAction` adds a definition; `listActions` returns all registered, grouped by entityType
Action registry: duplicate action name throws on register (or last-wins; pick and test)
Bootstrap loads all v1 package action contributions before first inbound request
Field mapping evaluator: JSONata expression evaluated correctly against request body
Field mapping: malformed JSONata → failed delivery, not 500
Field mapping: missing required target field → failed delivery with clear error
Lookup-miss in any `*ByExternalId` action → failed delivery with `lookup_miss` reason
`createTicket` action creates ticket with mapped fields; tenant scoped
`createTicket` writes mapping row when an `external_id` is mapped
`createTicket` records ticket_id on delivery row
`updateTicketByExternalId` resolves ticket via mappings table and updates status/priority/assignee
`updateTicketByExternalId` no mapping found → failed delivery
`addTicketCommentByExternalId` appends comment to resolved ticket
`changeTicketStatusByExternalId` transitions ticket through valid status only (invalid status → failed delivery)
`upsertClientByExternalId` creates client when mapping absent and writes mapping row
`upsertClientByExternalId` updates client when mapping resolves
`setClientActiveByExternalId` toggles active state correctly
`upsertContactByExternalId` creates or updates a client_user record
`upsertContactByExternalId` requires a client linkage (either mapped client_external_id or direct client_id)
`upsertAssetByExternalId` for RMM-shaped payload delegates to `ingestNormalizedRmmDeviceSnapshot`
RMM-path asset upsert produces identical record shape to NinjaOne/Tactical RMM ingestion (regression vs sample)
`upsertAssetByExternalId` for non-RMM payload takes plain asset upsert path
`markInvoicePaidByExternalId` marks matching invoice paid
`markInvoicePaidByExternalId` lookup-miss → failed delivery
`markInvoicePaidByExternalId` is idempotent: re-applying to already-paid invoice does not double-mark or error
`updateInvoiceStatusByExternalId` updates invoice status via mapped value
`createTimeEntry` creates a time entry with mapped user, work item, duration, billable flag
`createProjectTask` creates a task under a project resolved by external_id or direct project_id
`updateProjectTaskStatusByExternalId` updates status via mappings lookup
`addTagToEntityByExternalId` attaches tag to ticket when entity_type=ticket
`addTagToEntityByExternalId` attaches tag to client when entity_type=client
`addTagToEntityByExternalId` rejects unsupported entity_type with clear error
Workflow handler starts a workflow run with the documented envelope shape

Envelope `headers` is filtered (no Cookie/Authorization)
Delivery row stores workflow_run_id after successful trigger
Workflow engine error on trigger → failed delivery with error message
Workflow run failure AFTER trigger does NOT mark delivery failed

ExpressionTextArea autocompletes paths from adapter

No hardcoded English strings in new UI components (i18n scan passes)
All interactive elements have kebab-case `id` attributes (UI reflection scan passes)
Feature flag off: Settings tab hidden

`GET /api/v1/inbound-webhooks` returns 200 + list scoped to tenant
`POST /api/v1/inbound-webhooks` creates config and returns 201 + secret (once)
`GET /api/v1/inbound-webhooks/{id}` returns config without secret
`PUT /api/v1/inbound-webhooks/{id}` updates fields and persists
`DELETE /api/v1/inbound-webhooks/{id}` returns 204
`POST /api/v1/inbound-webhooks/{id}/rotate-secret` returns new secret once
`POST /api/v1/inbound-webhooks/{id}/test` dispatches synthetic request in-process
`POST /api/v1/inbound-webhooks/{id}/capture-sample` enables capture window
`DELETE /api/v1/inbound-webhooks/{id}/capture-sample` clears captured sample
`GET /api/v1/inbound-webhooks/{id}/deliveries` paginates and filters
`GET /api/v1/inbound-webhooks/{id}/deliveries/{deliveryId}` returns full delivery
`POST .../deliveries/{deliveryId}/replay` creates new delivery row linked to original
`GET /api/v1/inbound-webhooks/actions` returns all registered actions with target field schemas
Action discovery endpoint output matches registry contents (no drift)
